Yazeed Abulaila.bio: Young Jordanian goalkeeper who has risen through the ranks at Al-Ramtha to become a trusted backup for the national team. Known for his shot-stopping reflexes and commanding presence in the box.
Yazeed Abulaila.playingStyle: Athletic keeper with strong reflexes and confident handling of crosses
Yazeed Abulaila.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper providing depth behind more experienced options
Yazeed Abulaila.notableFact: Was part of Jordan's historic run to the 2024 AFC Asian Cup final

Ahmad Haikal.bio: Experienced right-back who has been a steady presence for Al-Wehdat and the Jordanian national team. His 45 caps demonstrate his reliability and consistency at international level.
Ahmad Haikal.playingStyle: Disciplined defender who prioritizes positional awareness and solid tackling over attacking runs
Ahmad Haikal.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back or experienced backup providing defensive stability
Ahmad Haikal.notableFact: Has spent his entire professional career in the Jordan Pro League, becoming one of the most capped defenders at Al-Wehdat

Abdallah Nasib.bio: Veteran center-back and Al-Faisaly stalwart who brings experience and leadership to the Jordanian defense. With 55 caps and 3 goals, he has been a regular in the national setup for years.
Abdallah Nasib.playingStyle: Commanding aerial presence with good reading of the game and ability to organize the backline
Abdallah Nasib.worldCup2026Role: Senior center-back option providing experience and mentorship in the defensive unit
Abdallah Nasib.notableFact: One of the longest-serving players at Al-Faisaly, one of Jordan's most historic clubs

Anas Bani-Yaseen.bio: One of Jordan's most experienced defenders with 80 international caps. The Al-Faisaly center-back has been a cornerstone of the national team defense for over a decade, contributing 5 goals from set pieces.
Anas Bani-Yaseen.playingStyle: Physical, no-nonsense defender who excels in aerial duels and is a threat from corners and free kicks
Anas Bani-Yaseen.worldCup2026Role: Veteran defensive leader and set-piece specialist, likely starting center-back
Anas Bani-Yaseen.notableFact: At 37, he would be one of the oldest players at the 2026 World Cup, bringing invaluable experience to Jordan's first-ever appearance

Yazan Al-Arab.bio: Versatile center-back from Al-Wehdat who has established himself as a key part of Jordan's defensive setup. At 30, he is in the prime of his career with 35 caps to his name.
Yazan Al-Arab.playingStyle: Ball-playing center-back comfortable with distribution and capable of stepping into midfield
Yazan Al-Arab.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back who helps build play from the back
Yazan Al-Arab.notableFact: Part of the Al-Wehdat squad that has dominated Jordanian football in recent seasons

Ehsan Haddad.bio: Defensive midfielder from Al-Wehdat who provides a shield for the backline. His 25 caps reflect his growing importance to the national team setup.
Ehsan Haddad.playingStyle: Tenacious ball-winner who breaks up opposition attacks and keeps possession ticking over
Ehsan Haddad.worldCup2026Role: Midfield anchor providing defensive cover and allowing more creative players to push forward
Ehsan Haddad.notableFact: Part of the strong Al-Wehdat contingent that forms the spine of the Jordanian national team

Mousa Al-Taamari.bio: Jordan's star player and most valuable asset, plying his trade in Ligue 1 with Rennes. The tricky left winger is the creative spark and talisman of the national team with 12 goals in 55 caps.
Mousa Al-Taamari.playingStyle: Explosive winger with dazzling dribbling ability, quick feet, and an eye for goal from wide positions
Mousa Al-Taamari.worldCup2026Role: Primary attacking threat and creative outlet on the left wing, the player opponents will plan to stop
Mousa Al-Taamari.notableFact: The highest-valued Jordanian player in history, he became the first Jordanian to play in a top-five European league

Baha Faisal.bio: Experienced central midfielder and Al-Wehdat captain who brings composure and leadership to Jordan's engine room. His 50 caps and 3 goals underline his importance to the team.
Baha Faisal.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder with good stamina, technical ability, and the vision to dictate tempo
Baha Faisal.worldCup2026Role: Starting central midfielder providing balance between defense and attack
Baha Faisal.notableFact: Has been one of the most influential players in the Jordan Pro League over the past decade

Hamza Al-Dardour.bio: Jordan's all-time leading scorer with 32 goals in 100 caps, the veteran striker is a living legend of Jordanian football. Despite his age, his predatory instincts remain razor-sharp.
Hamza Al-Dardour.playingStyle: Clinical poacher with excellent positioning, aerial ability, and finishing in the penalty area
Hamza Al-Dardour.worldCup2026Role: Iconic captain and leader, likely starting striker whose experience will be invaluable on the World Cup stage
Hamza Al-Dardour.notableFact: Became Jordan's first centurion and all-time top scorer, a true legend who will lead his country into their first World Cup

Ahmad Ersan.bio: One of Jordan's most decorated midfielders with 90 caps and 10 goals. The Al-Wehdat playmaker has been a consistent performer for over a decade and brings vital experience.
Ahmad Ersan.playingStyle: Creative midfielder with excellent passing, vision, and set-piece delivery
Ahmad Ersan.worldCup2026Role: Experienced midfield option who can unlock defenses with his passing and set-piece quality
Ahmad Ersan.notableFact: One of the few Jordanian players to reach 90 caps, making him a pillar of the national team's golden generation

Yazan Al-Naimat.bio: Exciting young striker who earned a move to Ligue 1 with Angers after impressive performances for Jordan. With 11 goals in 30 caps, he is the future of Jordanian attacking football.
Yazan Al-Naimat.playingStyle: Dynamic, mobile striker who combines pace with clinical finishing and intelligent movement
Yazan Al-Naimat.worldCup2026Role: Key striker alongside or competing with Al-Dardour for the starting role up front
Yazan Al-Naimat.notableFact: One of only two Jordanian players in Europe's top five leagues, making the move to France to further his career

Ali Olwan.bio: Versatile forward from Al-Salt who provides depth to Jordan's attacking options. With 5 goals in 20 caps, he offers a reliable alternative in the forward line.
Ali Olwan.playingStyle: Hard-working forward who presses defenders and creates space for teammates with intelligent runs
Ali Olwan.worldCup2026Role: Squad rotation option in attack, capable of making an impact off the bench
Ali Olwan.notableFact: Emerged from the smaller Al-Salt club to earn a place in Jordan's historic World Cup squad

Abdulla Rawashdeh.bio: Experienced right winger from Al-Faisaly who adds width and directness to Jordan's attack. His 35 caps and 3 goals reflect his steady contribution at international level.
Abdulla Rawashdeh.playingStyle: Direct winger who hugs the touchline, delivers crosses, and uses his pace to beat defenders
Abdulla Rawashdeh.worldCup2026Role: Attacking option from the right wing, providing width and delivery into the box
Abdulla Rawashdeh.notableFact: A product of the Al-Faisaly academy, one of the most successful youth development programs in Jordan

Salem Al-Ajalin.bio: Young left-back from Al-Ahli who brings energy and attacking intent from the defensive position. A natural left-footer, he provides balance to Jordan's backline.
Salem Al-Ajalin.playingStyle: Overlapping full-back who combines defensive solidity with the ability to get forward and deliver crosses
Salem Al-Ajalin.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back whose natural left foot and energy give Jordan width in attack
Salem Al-Ajalin.notableFact: One of the youngest defenders in the squad, representing the next generation of Jordanian talent

Yosif Al-Kalaldeh.bio: Young center-back from Al-Wehdat who is developing into a reliable option for the national team. Still early in his international career with 15 caps, he represents the future of Jordan's defense.
Yosif Al-Kalaldeh.playingStyle: Tall, composed defender who reads the game well and is comfortable playing out from the back
Yosif Al-Kalaldeh.worldCup2026Role: Backup center-back who can step in and provide quality defensive depth
Yosif Al-Kalaldeh.notableFact: Part of the talented Al-Wehdat defensive unit that has produced several international players

Qusai Al-Tamari.bio: Young midfielder playing in the Israeli Premier League with Hapoel Be'er Sheva, one of the few Jordanians plying their trade abroad. His move abroad has accelerated his development.
Qusai Al-Tamari.playingStyle: Technical midfielder with good ball control, creative passing, and an ability to find pockets of space
Qusai Al-Tamari.worldCup2026Role: Emerging midfield talent who provides a creative spark from the bench
Qusai Al-Tamari.notableFact: One of the youngest members of the squad at 25, gaining valuable experience playing competitively outside Jordan

Nizar Al-Rashdan.bio: Experienced defensive midfielder from Al-Wehdat with 45 caps to his name. A tough-tackling presence in the middle of the park who has been a loyal servant to Jordanian football.
Nizar Al-Rashdan.playingStyle: Robust defensive midfielder who excels at winning the ball back and maintaining the team's defensive shape
Nizar Al-Rashdan.worldCup2026Role: Experienced midfield option providing defensive solidity and cover when needed
Nizar Al-Rashdan.notableFact: Has been a fixture in the Jordan midfield for over a decade, rarely missing a competitive campaign

Mohannad Al-Namat.bio: Exciting young winger from Al-Arabi who adds pace and trickery to Jordan's attacking options. At 25, he is still developing and eager to make his mark on the international stage.
Mohannad Al-Namat.playingStyle: Quick, direct winger who takes on defenders with skill moves and has a dangerous left-foot shot
Mohannad Al-Namat.worldCup2026Role: Young attacking option off the bench, capable of stretching tired defenses late in matches
Mohannad Al-Namat.notableFact: Represents the emerging generation of Jordanian talent coming through the domestic league system

Jihad Al-Bahadri.bio: Young central midfielder from Al-Ramtha who has earned 10 caps for Jordan. A promising talent who adds energy and dynamism to the midfield options.
Jihad Al-Bahadri.playingStyle: Energetic box-to-box midfielder who covers ground quickly and contributes in both phases of play
Jihad Al-Bahadri.worldCup2026Role: Young squad player providing midfield depth and energy from the bench
Jihad Al-Bahadri.notableFact: One of the breakout players from Al-Ramtha's recent domestic success

Amr Abujabel.bio: Experienced goalkeeper from Al-Ahli who serves as a reliable backup in the national team. With 15 caps, he provides seasoned competition for the goalkeeping position.
Amr Abujabel.playingStyle: Commanding goalkeeper with good shot-stopping and vocal organization of the defense
Amr Abujabel.worldCup2026Role: Second-choice goalkeeper providing experienced competition and backup
Amr Abujabel.notableFact: One of the most experienced goalkeepers in the Jordan Pro League, having played top-flight football for over a decade

Hassan Al-Tamari.bio: Young right winger from Al-Faisaly who brings pace and directness to Jordan's flanks. With 12 caps and 2 goals, he is establishing himself in the international setup.
Hassan Al-Tamari.playingStyle: Pacy winger who likes to cut inside and shoot, or deliver crosses from wide positions
Hassan Al-Tamari.worldCup2026Role: Attacking squad option on the right wing, capable of changing games with his speed
Hassan Al-Tamari.notableFact: Shares the Al-Tamari surname with star player Mousa Al-Taamari, though they represent different clubs domestically

Baha' Abdulrahman.bio: Young goalkeeper from Al-Faisaly who rounds out Jordan's goalkeeping contingent. With 5 caps, he is the least experienced of the three keepers but has a promising future.
Baha' Abdulrahman.playingStyle: Agile goalkeeper with quick reflexes and improving distribution skills
Baha' Abdulrahman.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper gaining experience from being part of the World Cup squad
Baha' Abdulrahman.notableFact: A product of the Al-Faisaly academy, one of the strongest goalkeeper development programs in Jordan

Yazan Tawaha.bio: Young right-back from Al-Salt who provides defensive depth. With 8 caps, he is still early in his international career but has shown promise in domestic competition.
Yazan Tawaha.playingStyle: Solid, dependable right-back who focuses on defensive duties and simple distribution
Yazan Tawaha.worldCup2026Role: Backup right-back providing squad depth in the defensive positions
Yazan Tawaha.notableFact: Has developed his career at Al-Salt, one of the smaller clubs in the Jordan Pro League

Obada Al-Horani.bio: The youngest outfield player in Jordan's squad, this Al-Wehdat midfielder represents the future of Jordanian football. At just 23, his inclusion speaks to his immense potential.
Obada Al-Horani.playingStyle: Technically gifted young midfielder with good passing range and composure beyond his years
Obada Al-Horani.worldCup2026Role: Young talent gaining World Cup experience, potential impact substitute in midfield
Obada Al-Horani.notableFact: One of the youngest players in Jordan's historic first World Cup squad

Alaa Al-Dali.bio: Young center-back from Al-Faisaly who adds depth to Jordan's defensive options. With 5 caps, he is still building his international career but has shown solid performances domestically.
Alaa Al-Dali.playingStyle: Strong, physical center-back who competes well in aerial duels and makes clean tackles
Alaa Al-Dali.worldCup2026Role: Backup center-back providing squad depth in the defensive line
Alaa Al-Dali.notableFact: Part of the Al-Faisaly defensive school that has produced several of Jordan's international defenders

Firas Shelbaieh.bio: Winger based in Denmark's second tier with Esbjerg, one of the few Jordanian players in European football. His experience abroad gives him a different dimension compared to domestic-based teammates.
Firas Shelbaieh.playingStyle: Quick, tricky winger with an eye for goal and the ability to beat defenders in one-on-one situations
Firas Shelbaieh.worldCup2026Role: Impact substitute who can provide fresh attacking impetus from the bench
Firas Shelbaieh.notableFact: One of the few Jordanian players to have carved out a career in European football, bringing a different tactical perspective to the squad
